impact: T-088 can verify the browser workflow with local route mocks, but it cannot validate the real Apoth API boundary for `/api/onboarding/start`, `/api/onboarding/consent`, `/api/intake/bootstrap`, `/api/intake/precheck`, `/api/onboarding/mdi/bootstrap`, or `/api/onboarding/mdi/submit` because those route handlers are not present. A future implementation could leak questionnaire answers or mishandle billing/Stripe metadata without this browser-level mock suite catching the route-layer behavior.
resolution: Implemented real Next.js route handlers for /api/onboarding/start, /api/onboarding/consent, /api/intake/bootstrap, /api/intake/precheck, /api/onboarding/mdi/bootstrap, and /api/onboarding/mdi/submit. Added shared auth/origin/CSRF/no-store helpers, DynamoDB-backed intake profile and MDI intake adapters, and an MDI HTTP intake gateway with typed provider-unavailable mapping. Added focused route-boundary tests proving authenticated gating, same-origin/CSRF enforcement, minimal residency-only precheck persistence, patient-safe MDI outage responses, and no questionnaire-answer fragments in submit responses. Verification passed: npm test -- src/app/api/onboarding/__tests__/route-boundary.test.ts, npm run typecheck, npm test.
title: Real intake/onboarding API route handlers are missing behind mocked E2E flow
